title: Card C — Cross-Venue Lead-Lag (Perp/Spot → Binary Mid)
headline: n_binary_expiries=38; n_with_xcorr=35; corpus=2026-05-06..2026-06-10; perp_lead=sub-5s; contemp_corr_median=0.283; hedge_ratio_median=0.498

metrics.name: perp_to_binary_lead_time_median_s
metrics.sanity: 0s at 5s grid = sub-5s lead; binary responds to perp within same bucket. At 1s resolution, peak is still lag=0 confirming sub-second response.

metrics.name: perp_to_binary_response_halflife_s
metrics.sanity: lag at which xcorr falls to half of lag-0 value; shorter = faster decay = less predictability beyond 1 step

metrics.name: perp_spot_peak_lag_median_s
metrics.sanity: negative = perp leads spot; after 223ms latency correction

mm_implication: Binary responds to perp within the same 5s grid bucket (sub-second). Contemporaneous xcorr=0.283 with decay half-life=5s. An MM quoting the binary is adversely selected at sub-second cadence by takers who observe perp price movement first. Quote refresh must complete in <1s (not 5s or 30s). Hedge via HL perp is operationally viable: HL round-trip ~100-500ms, which fits within the ~1s window before the binary confirms the perp move. Hedge ratio ≈0.50 (∂binary_mid/∂model_prob from OLS; std=0.18).
verdict: Perp→binary lead time: SUB-5s (binary responds within same 5s bucket). Contemporaneous xcorr=0.283 (R²=0.080). Half-life=5s (decay from peak). Split-half stable. | HL perp vs Binance spot: contemporaneous at 5s (both venues at 5s grid, need <1s resolution to differentiate after latency correction). | Taker signal from perp-lag: R²=0.080, does NOT survive half-spread (conservative 0.005). At 5s grid the predictability beyond lag=0 is weak (R²≈0.03 at lag=+5s). | MM implication: MM must refresh quotes at sub-second cadence (not 5s). Hedge ratio≈0.50; stable across halves. HL perp hedge viable given ~100-500ms HL round-trip vs sub-1s adversarial window.
